title: What I learned in Hue
summary: A Dutch architect comes to Hue for one week to study old wooden houses. She stays for one month after one sentence from an old carpenter.
body:
My name is Anneke. I am an architect from the Netherlands. Last summer I came to Hue, in central Vietnam. I came for one week to see the old wooden houses. I stayed for one month. I will explain why.

Dutch houses are tall and narrow. We have small gardens behind. Our walls are thick. Our windows are small. We build for the cold. We build for the rain. We build to keep heat inside.

The houses in Hue are different. They are low. They are wide. The roofs are large and dark. The walls are thin wood. The doors are open. Sometimes there are no doors — only spaces. The houses do not keep heat inside. They send heat out.

On my first day, I sat with an old carpenter. He was eighty-one. He has built these houses since he was fourteen. He drew the plan of one house on the floor with chalk.

'Why is the roof so big?' I asked.

'For the rain,' he said. 'And for the sun. The big roof gives shade to the wood. Wet wood and hot wood both die. The roof protects the house from itself.'

I wrote in my notebook: 'A roof can protect a house from itself.' I came to see houses. I learned a sentence.

That is why I stayed one month.

annotations.meaningVi: bảo vệ (động từ ngôi ba số ít)
cuốn sổ tay, vở ghi chép
thoát nhiệt ra ngoài (đối lập với 'keep heat inside')
gỗ mỏng (mô tả chất liệu và độ dày của tường)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'since'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại hoàn thành ('has/have + V3') với 'since + mốc thời gian' để nói một việc bắt đầu từ một thời điểm trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp tục. 'He has built these houses since he was fourteen.' (Bắt đầu từ năm mười bốn tuổi, đến giờ vẫn làm.) Sau 'since' là một mốc cụ thể: 'since 2010', 'since I was a child', 'since last summer'. So sánh 'for + khoảng thời gian': 'for ten years'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect with 'since + a point in time' to show an action started in the past and continues now. 'He has built these houses since he was fourteen.' After 'since' you put a specific point: 'since 2010', 'since I was a child', 'since last summer'. Compare with 'for + a length of time': 'for ten years'.

translationVi:
Tôi tên là Anneke. Tôi là một kiến trúc sư đến từ Hà Lan. Mùa hè năm ngoái tôi đến Huế, miền Trung Việt Nam. Tôi đến một tuần để xem những ngôi nhà gỗ cổ. Tôi ở lại một tháng. Tôi sẽ giải thích vì sao.

Nhà Hà Lan cao và hẹp. Phía sau có khu vườn nhỏ. Tường dày. Cửa sổ nhỏ. Chúng tôi xây để chống lạnh. Chúng tôi xây để chống mưa. Chúng tôi xây để giữ hơi ấm bên trong.

Nhà ở Huế thì khác. Nhà thấp. Nhà rộng. Mái lớn và sẫm màu. Tường gỗ mỏng. Cửa mở. Có khi không có cửa — chỉ có khoảng trống. Nhà không giữ hơi ấm bên trong. Nhà thoát hơi nóng ra ngoài.

Ngày đầu, tôi ngồi với một ông thợ mộc già. Ông tám mươi mốt tuổi. Ông làm những ngôi nhà này từ năm mười bốn tuổi. Ông vẽ bằng phấn lên sàn sơ đồ một ngôi nhà.

'Sao mái lại to thế ạ?' tôi hỏi.

'Vì mưa,' ông đáp. 'Và vì nắng. Mái lớn cho bóng râm xuống gỗ. Gỗ ướt và gỗ nóng đều chết. Mái bảo vệ ngôi nhà khỏi chính nó.'

Tôi viết vào sổ: 'Mái có thể bảo vệ một ngôi nhà khỏi chính nó.' Tôi đến để xem nhà. Tôi học được một câu.

Đó là lý do tôi ở lại một tháng.

questions.prompt: Who is the writer of the passage?
questions.explanation: 'My name is Anneke. I am an architect from the Netherlands.'

questions.prompt: Why did she come to Hue?
options: To see the old wooden houses, for one week
questions.explanation: 'I came for one week to see the old wooden houses.'

questions.prompt: How does she describe Dutch houses?
options: Tall and narrow, thick walls, small windows — built to keep heat inside
Low and wide, with big roofs
Always with two floors and a swimming pool
questions.explanation: 'Dutch houses are tall and narrow… We build to keep heat inside.'

questions.prompt: How are the houses in Hue different?
options: Low, wide, large dark roofs, thin walls, sometimes no doors — and they send heat out.
Tall and narrow with brick walls.

questions.prompt: How long has he been building these houses?
questions.explanation: 'He has built these houses since he was fourteen.'

questions.prompt: How does the carpenter draw the plan?
options: He does not draw — he speaks only.
questions.explanation: 'He drew the plan of one house on the floor with chalk.'

questions.prompt: Why is the roof so big, according to the carpenter?
options: For the rain and the sun — to give shade so the wet wood and hot wood do not 'die.'
Because old people prefer big roofs.
questions.explanation: 'For the rain. And for the sun. The big roof gives shade to the wood. Wet wood and hot wood both die.'

questions.prompt: What sentence does Anneke write in her notebook?
options: 'A roof can protect a house from itself.'
questions.explanation: She quotes the carpenter's idea and writes it down.
questions.hint: Look for the line in the notebook.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: A short visit can become a long one when one sentence changes how you see your own profession.
Dutch houses are better than Vietnamese houses.
Wood is the best material everywhere.
Hue is the largest city in Vietnam.
questions.explanation: Anneke planned a week and stayed a month — because of what she learned from one sentence.
questions.hint: Think about why she stays for a month.

'For the rain,' he said. 'And for the sun. The big roof gives {{b9}} to the wood. Wet wood and hot wood both die. The roof protects the house from itself.'

blanks.explanation: Dutch houses are tall and narrow.
The roofs are large and dark.
He drew with chalk on the floor.
The big roof gives shade to the wood.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The carpenter's sentence — 'a roof protects a house from itself' — was useful far beyond houses. Is there a sentence from a stranger that has stayed with you? What did it really mean, beyond its first subject?
